title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
As Jiles Reed and Gwendolyn prepare to travel to Exeter to meet J.J. Avery, the owner of the Water Lily Carriage Company, Jiles receives a phone call from Dr. Kennedy. Dr. Kennedy reports receiving a strange letter from Lily Kimball, a former maid in his household. In the letter, Lily includes a clipping and expresses doubts regarding a past event involving a woman named Mrs. Harriet. Lily also mentions seeing a fancy car outside and suspects Mr. Kimball may be involved, though she expresses fear regarding police involvement. Dr. Kennedy arranges for Lily to visit on a Thursday so they can discuss the matter together.

During this conversation, Dr. Kennedy mentions that he recognizes the name Avery. He recalls that Jackie Avery was previously dismissed from a firm for attempting to steal confidential information to sell for profit. Kennedy notes that following his dismissal, Avery exhibited emotional instability and signs of persecution delusion.

Jiles and Gwendolyn eventually meet J.J. Avery at his luxurious new residence. Avery is a middle-aged, plump man with shrewd black eyes. When Jiles attempts to discuss a potential business trip for a boys' club, Avery questions the need for a private meeting. Jiles explains that Gwendolyn wishes to reconnect with her stepmother, Helen Harley, formerly known as Helen Kennedy. Upon hearing the name, Avery reacts with sudden excitement, claiming to remember "Little Miss Helen Kennedy" from eighteen years ago, though he asserts he has had no news of her since that time.
